constraints.analysis_summary: Round 1 provided no information because the guess was empty. No constraints can be derived. The secret remains completely unknown.

Round 2 provides that exactly 3 of the 4 guessed colors (red, blue, green, yellow) are in the secret, all misplaced relative to their guess positions. One of these four is absent. The fourth secret color is unknown. No locked positions yet.

Round 3 combined with prior rounds: We have two main scenarios. Scenario A: Both green and yellow are present (so red and blue are present? Actually if both green and yellow, then the three from S must be either {red, green, yellow} or {blue, green, yellow}; so either red or blue is absent). In this scenario, white and black are impossible. Scenario B: Only one of green/yellow present (say green present, yellow absent), then the three from S must be {red, blue, green} (since yellow absent, red and blue must be present to make three). Then exactly one of white/black is present to account for the 2 pegs in Round 3. The other is impossible. Similarly if yellow present and green absent, then three from S are {red, blue, yellow}. No locked positions yet.

Round 4, combined with prior rounds, gives two possible scenarios: (A) Secret contains yellow, green, and either red or blue, plus a duplicate; white absent, black absent. (B) Secret contains yellow, red, blue, and black; white and green absent. Misplacement constraints restrict positions: red ≠0, blue ≠1, green ≠0,2, yellow ≠1,3, black ≠3. No locked positions yet.

After Round 5, we have determined that the secret contains two yellows, one green, and exactly one of red or blue. White and black are impossible. Yellow must be at positions 0 and 2 (deduced from misplacement constraints). Green and the remaining color (red or blue) occupy positions 1 and 3 in three possible orders (red at 1 or 3, blue only at 3). No locked positions from direct matches.

After Round 6, the secret is fully resolved: [Yellow, Red, Yellow, Green]. All prior constraints are satisfied. Round 6 confirmed that yellow must be at positions 0 and 2, green at position 3, and red at position 1. No further guesses needed.
Round 7 confirms the secret deduced from prior rounds: [Y, R, Y, G]. All feedback is perfectly consistent. No further guesses needed.
Round 8 confirms the known solution [Y, R, Y, G]. All prior constraints are satisfied. The secret has been fully identified. No further guesses are needed.

reasoning_steps: Step 1: IDENTIFY EXISTING COLORS - The guess has two greens and two yellows. Pegs=3. From prior analysis, we know secret has 2 yellows and 1 green (and one of red/blue). The peg count: min(2, n_g) + min(2, n_y) = 3. With n_y=2, min(2,2)=2, so need min(2, n_g)=1 => n_g=1. So green and yellow are confirmed present, consistent with prior.

Step 2: IDENTIFY LOCKED POSITIONS - Two correct positions (positions=2). Comparing guess [G,Y,Y,G] to known secret (from prior rounds) [Y,R,Y,G] (deduced after solving all previous constraints), the matches are at pos2 (guess Y matches secret Y) and pos3 (guess G matches secret G). Thus locked positions: position 2 is yellow, position 3 is green.

Step 3: IDENTIFY MISPLACED COLORS - One misplaced peg (pegs - positions = 1). The three pegs consist of: one yellow at pos2 (correct), one yellow at pos1 (misplaced), and one green at pos3 (correct). So yellow at position 1 is misplaced, consistent with prior constraints (yellow cannot be at 1 or 3).
Step 4: IDENTIFY IMPOSSIBLE COLORS - No new impossible colors from this round. White and black were already impossible from prior rounds.
Step 5: IDENTIFY UNKNOWNS - The full secret is determined: position 0 is yellow, position 1 is red, position 2 is yellow, position 3 is green. No unknowns remain.
analysis_result.analysis: After Round 6, the secret is fully resolved: [Yellow, Red, Yellow, Green]. All prior constraints are satisfied. Round 6 confirmed that yellow must be at positions 0 and 2, green at position 3, and red at position 1. No further guesses needed.
constraints: Secret is exactly [Y, R, Y, G].

constraints.analysis_summary: Round 1 provided no information because the guess was empty. No constraints can be derived. The secret remains completely unknown.

Round 2 provides that exactly 3 of the 4 guessed colors (red, blue, green, yellow) are in the secret, all misplaced relative to their guess positions. One of these four is absent. The fourth secret color is unknown. No locked positions yet.

Round 3 combined with prior rounds: We have two main scenarios. Scenario A: Both green and yellow are present (so red and blue are present? Actually if both green and yellow, then the three from S must be either {red, green, yellow} or {blue, green, yellow}; so either red or blue is absent). In this scenario, white and black are impossible. Scenario B: Only one of green/yellow present (say green present, yellow absent), then the three from S must be {red, blue, green} (since yellow absent, red and blue must be present to make three). Then exactly one of white/black is present to account for the 2 pegs in Round 3. The other is impossible. Similarly if yellow present and green absent, then three from S are {red, blue, yellow}. No locked positions yet.

Round 4, combined with prior rounds, gives two possible scenarios: (A) Secret contains yellow, green, and either red or blue, plus a duplicate; white absent, black absent. (B) Secret contains yellow, red, blue, and black; white and green absent. Misplacement constraints restrict positions: red ≠0, blue ≠1, green ≠0,2, yellow ≠1,3, black ≠3. No locked positions yet.

After Round 5, we have determined that the secret contains two yellows, one green, and exactly one of red or blue. White and black are impossible. Yellow must be at positions 0 and 2 (deduced from misplacement constraints). Green and the remaining color (red or blue) occupy positions 1 and 3 in three possible orders (red at 1 or 3, blue only at 3). No locked positions from direct matches.

After Round 6, the secret is fully resolved: [Yellow, Red, Yellow, Green]. All prior constraints are satisfied. Round 6 confirmed that yellow must be at positions 0 and 2, green at position 3, and red at position 1. No further guesses needed.
Round 7 confirms the secret deduced from prior rounds: [Y, R, Y, G]. All feedback is perfectly consistent. No further guesses needed.
Round 8 confirms the known solution [Y, R, Y, G]. All prior constraints are satisfied. The secret has been fully identified. No further guesses are needed.
